nodes.context: A Jewish criminal mob of bootleggers and hijackers operating in Detroit during the 1920s Prohibition era.
Organized crime syndicate based in Chicago, led by Al Capone during the Prohibition era.
Italian bootlegging gang led by Tommy and Pete Licavoli, rival of the Purple Gang in Detroit.
St. Louis-based criminal gang associated with Fred Burke.
Sicilian Mafia organization in Detroit, successor to the Purple Gang's dominance.

edges.evidence_quote: Chicago gangster Al Capone was against expanding his rackets in Detroit, so he began a business accommodation with the Purple Gang in order to prevent a bloody war. For several years, the gang managed the prosperous business of supplying Canadian whisky to the Capone organization in Chicago.
edges.context: The Purple Gang supplied Canadian whisky to the Chicago Outfit under a business accommodation.

edges.evidence_quote: The Purples fought a vicious turf war with the Licavoli Squad led by the brothers Tommy and Pete Licavoli.
edges.context: The Purple Gang fought a turf war with the Licavoli Squad.
The Detroit Partnership is listed as a rival of the Purple Gang in the infobox.
